_comment: Ambulance cover by Australian state and territory — costs and coverage rules
_note: Ambulance fees vary by distance, call type, and circumstances. Costs listed are typical emergency transport charges. Residents of free states are covered within their state only — interstate travel may incur costs.
importantNotes: Ambulance cover within PHI (extras or hospital cover) typically covers emergency and medically necessary transport
Even in 'free' states, cover only applies to state residents — interstate travel is not automatically covered
QLD and TAS residents still benefit from ambulance cover in PHI for interstate travel
VIC concession card holders receive free ambulance — check eligibility before purchasing cover

QLD.coverageDescription: Free ambulance service for all Queensland residents — funded by the state government via the Queensland Ambulance Service levy
QLD.levyInclusion: Included in electricity bills for most households

costWithoutCover.description: No charge for QLD residents receiving QAS services within Queensland

QLD.phiRecommendation: Low priority for residents only travelling within QLD. Consider ambulance cover if travelling interstate regularly.
QLD.interstateCoverage: Interstate transport NOT covered by QAS — PHI ambulance cover recommended for interstate travellers

TAS.coverageDescription: Free ambulance service for all Tasmanian residents provided by Ambulance Tasmania

costWithoutCover.description: No charge for TAS residents receiving Ambulance Tasmania services within Tasmania

TAS.phiRecommendation: Low priority for residents only travelling within TAS. Consider ambulance cover for interstate travel.
TAS.interstateCoverage: Interstate transport NOT covered — PHI ambulance cover recommended for interstate travellers

VIC.partialCoverageDescription: Free for concession card holders (Centrelink health care cards, pension concession cards, DVA gold cards). Others must pay or subscribe.

subscriptionCost.description: Ambulance Victoria subscription — covers unlimited emergency call-outs
subscriptionCost.note: Subscription rate as at 2026. Cover applies statewide.

emergency.description: Emergency ambulance transport: approximately $1,282+ depending on distance and treatment

VIC.phiRecommendation: Important for non-concession VIC residents unless subscribing to Ambulance Victoria separately
VIC.interstateCoverage: Interstate transport not covered by AV subscription — PHI cover recommended

emergency.description: Emergency ambulance: $401 for local transport, up to $6,571+ for long-distance or air transport. Fixed flag fall + per-kilometre charges.

NSW.phiRecommendation: High priority — NSW has among the highest ambulance costs in Australia. Air ambulance can exceed $10,000.

emergency.description: Emergency ambulance transport: approximately $1,046 for standard road transport. Air transport significantly higher.
emergency.note: Per-kilometre charge applies beyond a standard base distance

SA.phiRecommendation: High priority for SA residents without concession card

WA.partialCoverageDescription: Free for Pensioner Concession Card holders, children under 3, and some other groups. St John WA operates ambulance services.

emergency.description: Emergency ambulance transport: typically $943–$2,000+ depending on distance. WA is geographically vast — remote area transport can be very expensive.

airTransport.description: Air ambulance (RFDS) can cost $10,000–$50,000+ for remote transport

WA.phiRecommendation: High priority — especially in regional/remote WA where air transport costs can be extreme
WA.interstateCoverage: Interstate transport not covered by St John subscription

ACT.partialCoverageDescription: Complex arrangement — ACT uses NSW Ambulance. Concession card holders may have reduced charges.

emergency.description: ACT uses NSW Ambulance Service — charges similar to NSW. Emergency transport from $401, can be much higher for complex cases.

NT.partialCoverageDescription: Partial subsidies apply for NT residents. St John Ambulance NT operates services. Concession holders have reduced charges.

emergency.description: Emergency ground transport approximately $636. Air transport (RFDS) costs dramatically higher in remote NT — can exceed $15,000–$30,000.

airTransport.description: Royal Flying Doctor Service (RFDS) air transport for remote areas can cost $15,000–$30,000+

NT.phiRecommendation: High priority — NT has significant remote areas where RFDS air transport is required. Costs can be extreme.
